name: union_always_empty
code:
proof fn phi_1_union_always_empty(pre_self: CommitMask, other: CommitMask, post_self: CommitMask)
    requires
        post_self@ == pre_self@.union(other@),
    ensures
        post_self@ =~= Set::<int>::empty(),
{
}
reason: Union should not always produce an empty set; this would mean set() discards all information.

name: loses_original_elements
code:
proof fn phi_2_loses_original_elements(pre_self: CommitMask, other: CommitMask, post_self: CommitMask, i: int)
    requires
        post_self@ == pre_self@.union(other@),
        pre_self@.contains(i),
        !other@.contains(i),
    ensures
        !post_self@.contains(i),
{
}
reason: An element present in old(self) but not in other must still be in the result; losing it means set() is not a true union.

name: result_equals_only_other
code:
proof fn phi_3_result_equals_only_other(pre_self: CommitMask, other: CommitMask, post_self: CommitMask)
    requires
        post_self@ == pre_self@.union(other@),
    ensures
        post_self@ =~= other@,
{
}
reason: The result equalling only `other@` would mean set() overwrites rather than unions, dropping all pre-existing bits.

name: union_not_idempotent
code:
proof fn phi_4_union_not_idempotent(m: CommitMask, post: CommitMask)
    requires
        post@ == m@.union(m@),
    ensures
        !(post@ =~= m@),
{
}
reason: Union of a mask with itself must equal itself (idempotence); failure indicates the spec allows set() to corrupt data in the self-union case.

name: spurious_element_added
code:
proof fn phi_5_spurious_element_added(pre_self: CommitMask, other: CommitMask, post_self: CommitMask, i: int)
    requires
        post_self@ == pre_self@.union(other@),
        !pre_self@.contains(i),
        !other@.contains(i),
    ensures
        post_self@.contains(i),
{
}
reason: An element absent from both operands must not appear in the result; its presence would mean set() introduces phantom bits.
